mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, increasing the present value of future cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 45% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a statistically significant positive mean return over 60 days (t-stat < 1.5), or the interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly growth rate in industrial production falling below 0.5% during a late-cycle expansion reveals supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns do not materialize when industrial production growth slows below the threshold, or the signal performs equally well in early-cycle phases, invalidating the regime dependency.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate market repricing of inflation expectations, forcing investors to discount equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the correlation between CPI spikes and short-term equity declines falls below -0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or flat 60-day forward return distribution, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of real gross domestic product reflect stronger aggregate economic activity that directly supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 levels remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP levels in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant industrial production alongside elevated consumer prices creates a stagflationary environment that forces rapid portfolio de-risking, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPI and low industrial production fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a success rate above 50%, or the mechanism loses predictive power when tested outside the specified regime.
